name: Zoho Meeting
h1: Build AI Agents That Work with Zoho Meeting
metaTitle: Build AI Agents for Zoho Meeting with DocsBot Skill Builder
metaDescription: Create DocsBot AI agents that use Zoho Meeting for scheduling lookups, session details, follow-up workflows, and internal coordination with safe actions.
intro: Zoho Meeting helps teams run online meetings, webinars, and remote collaboration workflows. With DocsBot Skill Builder, you can describe in plain language how you want an AI agent to use Zoho Meeting, and DocsBot helps design, secure, test, and deploy that ability. The result is a support or internal AI agent that can answer questions, look up meeting details, trigger approved follow-up actions, and keep teams moving without manual back-and-forth.
supportUseCases: Let a customer-facing DocsBot agent check upcoming meeting or webinar details in Zoho Meeting and reply with the correct time, joining guidance, or registration status.
Use the agent to create support handoffs after a customer asks for a live call, including preparing meeting context for the team before the session is scheduled.
Have the agent summarize recent meeting information from Zoho Meeting so support teams can respond to follow-up questions with better context.
Enable the agent to trigger approved post-meeting workflows, such as notifying a success manager or creating a next-step summary after a customer session.
Let the agent collect missing details from a customer in chat or a helpdesk ticket, then package that information for a live meeting handoff handled through Zoho Meeting.
internalUseCases: Employees can chat with a private DocsBot agent to find meeting links, webinar details, host information, or upcoming session schedules stored in Zoho Meeting.
Customer success or operations teams can ask the agent to prepare meeting summaries, participant context, and next-step notes for internal follow-up workflows.
Teams can use the agent in Slack, Microsoft Teams, or private chat to coordinate webinar logistics and retrieve the latest Zoho Meeting session details without switching tools.
Employees can ask the agent to follow SOPs around live customer calls, including gathering meeting details, preparing handoff notes, and triggering approved internal notifications.
Internal teams can use the agent to research session history and summarize what happened before escalating an issue or planning the next customer interaction.
whatYouCanBuild: An AI support agent that looks up Zoho Meeting session details during customer conversations.
A private assistant that retrieves webinar schedules and participant context for internal teams.
A handoff workflow that prepares live meeting notes before support escalations.
An agent that summarizes recent meeting activity and drafts next-step follow-up updates.
A support assistant that gathers customer details before a scheduled Zoho Meeting session.
An internal agent that triggers approved notifications after meetings or webinars end.

howItWorks.title: Describe the outcome
howItWorks.description: You tell Skill Builder what you want the Zoho Meeting agent to do in plain language, and it turns that goal into a practical support or internal workflow design.

howItWorks.title: Skill Builder designs safely
howItWorks.description: Skill Builder determines the right configuration values, connection method, and authentication setup, keeps secrets out of chats and source, proxies credentials securely at runtime, and scopes access to only the users, actions, and meeting data the workflow needs.

howItWorks.title: Bound actions and approvals
howItWorks.description: Skill Builder adds safe action limits and approval boundaries where needed so the agent can handle lookups, summaries, notifications, or record updates without exceeding the rules you set.

howItWorks.title: Test real scenarios
howItWorks.description: The automated testing agent runs imagined customer and employee scenarios against the Skill, finds bugs, weak instructions, missing checks, or workflow gaps, and feeds those findings back for improvement.

howItWorks.title: Improve and deploy
howItWorks.description: Skill Builder refines the Skill based on test results, prepares it for the right DocsBot agent, and helps publish it to your customer support chatbot, helpdesk agent, or private team assistant.

faq.question: How can I build an AI agent for Zoho Meeting?
faq.answer: With DocsBot Skill Builder, you describe the Zoho Meeting workflow you want in plain language, such as looking up meeting details, preparing handoffs, or triggering follow-up actions. Skill Builder then designs the Skill, secures the connection, tests scenarios, improves the workflow, and helps deploy it to the right DocsBot agent.

faq.question: Can a customer support AI agent use Zoho Meeting data during live chats or support tickets?
faq.answer: Yes, a DocsBot support agent can use a Zoho Meeting Skill to look up approved meeting or webinar details, summarize session context, and prepare live-call handoffs while responding to customers. The customer conversation triggers the DocsBot agent, and the Skill lets that agent safely work with Zoho Meeting behind the scenes.

faq.question: What Zoho Meeting workflows can an internal AI assistant automate for employees?
faq.answer: An internal DocsBot agent can help employees retrieve meeting links, check schedules, summarize session activity, prepare follow-up notes, and trigger approved coordination steps. This is useful in private chat, Slack, or Microsoft Teams when teams want faster access to live meeting information and related SOP workflows.

faq.question: Can DocsBot safely trigger actions in Zoho Meeting instead of only answering questions?
faq.answer: Yes, DocsBot Skills can be designed for bounded AI Actions such as approved notifications, handoff preparation, summaries, and related workflow triggers. Skill Builder defines secure configuration, scopes permissions, and adds approval boundaries for more sensitive actions.

faq.question: How does DocsBot handle authentication and security for a Zoho Meeting Skill?
faq.answer: Skill Builder handles the secure design by identifying required configuration values, protecting and proxying secrets through approved runtime configuration, and keeping credentials out of chat transcripts and generated Skill content. It also limits access to the minimum users, records, and actions needed for the workflow.

faq.question: Can an AI agent use Zoho Meeting to improve webinar and customer success coordination?
faq.answer: Yes, a DocsBot agent can gather webinar details, summarize session context, prepare next-step notes, and route internal follow-up work after a customer meeting or event. That helps support, success, and operations teams stay aligned without manually checking multiple tools.

brand.title: Zoho
brand.description: Zoho is a privately held software company headquartered in Chennai, India, that provides a comprehensive suite of over 55 cloud‑based applications for businesses of all sizes. Its products cover sales and marketing, customer support, collaboration, finance, and recruitment, helping more than 130 million users worldwide streamline operations and focus on growth. Zoho emphasizes user privacy, operates without an ad‑revenue model, and offers both free and paid tools designed to transform the way companies work.
brand.slogan: Zoho One: The operating system for business, uniting teams and efficiency.
